title: Graph Theory Concepts: Pendant Vertex, Null Graph, Paths, Circuits, and Isomorphism
description: A visual comparison of different graph types and concepts. It contrasts a simple graph with a pendant vertex against a null graph. It then illustrates a path and a circuit within a graph. Finally, it demonstrates graph isomorphism by showing two structurally identical graphs with different vertex labels.
elements: Graph G1 (with vertices A, B, C, D)
Graph G2 (Null Graph with 4 isolated vertices)
relationships: G1 contains a pendant vertex (D) connected only to C
G2 has no edges, representing a Null Graph
G3 is a subgraph of G1 showing a Path
G4 is a subgraph of G1 showing a Circuit
G1 and H1 are Isomorphic; they have the same structure but different vertex labels
